summary: Basket 13 catalyst sleeve — Catalyst-CRO trade attack (4 surfaces) then Director selection+sizing under hard caps
logs: Basket 13 — Catalyst-CRO: 19 candidates in 4 batches of 5 (opus)
CRO group 1 done; 19 verdicts so far

cro.live_edge_check: Live $24.75 (last close, NASDAQ via FMP batch-quote; mkt closed). Recomputed R:R from live: upside to FV $36 = +45.5%, downside to floor $8 = -67.7%. Dossier built at $23.02 (+56%/-65%). Edge compressed ~10pts on the upside leg but still well above half the dossier R:R, so EDGE INTACT. NOTE: FMP batch-quote name field mislabels DFTX as 'Mind Medicine' (stale name artifact, priceAvg50==priceAvg200==14.26 placeholder); search-symbol + 8 news items confirm DFTX = Definium Therapeutics, real entity, correct dossier mapping.
cro.tradeability_note: Equity is the clean expression: ADV ~1.7M shares today on a $2.7B-cap NASDAQ Global Select name, ample for a sleeve position. Options exist but I could not pull live bid/ask/OI in this read-only pass; equity is sufficient and avoids spread risk on a low-float biotech. If expressing via options, condition on a quoted June/July straddle with <15% effective spread.
cro.window_note: Dated milestone 2026-06-30 (DT120 ODT Phase 3 MDD topline 'later this quarter', confirmed by 2026-05-22 MarketBeat + CFO commentary), 17 days out. Equity carries no expiry risk. For options, the July monthly is the first expiry clearing the Jun-30 readout with margin (+1 monthly = +1 month past milestone). No public date-slip on this specific readout observed.
cro.driver_confirmed: FDA_clinical_readout — confirmed (Phase 3 topline, idiosyncratic single-asset binary). DUPLICATE-DRIVER FLAG for Director: EYPT in this same batch also resolves on FDA_clinical_readout / FDA-biotech super_cluster. Director should enforce the lane/driver cap across DFTX+EYPT.

cro.live_edge_check: Live $13.03 (last close, NASDAQ via FMP batch-quote; mkt closed). Recomputed R:R from live: upside to FV $37.5 = +187.8%, downside to floor $5.5 = -57.8%. cro.tradeability_note: Equity ADV ~1.1M shares on a $1.1B-cap NASDAQ name — tradeable for a sleeve clip. Single-asset binary; long-dated calls straddling the readout are the dossier-preferred expression but I could not confirm live OI/strikes near $37.5 FV / $5.5 floor in this pass. Condition any option leg on quoted OI > 500 at the chosen strike and effective spread < 15%.
cro.window_note: WINDOW SLIP RISK. Dossier milestone 2026-07-31, but latest company guidance (2026-05-26 MarketBeat / Goldman HC 2026-06-09) now puts LUGANO Phase 3 wet-AMD topline 'beginning in August 2026', with LUCIA ~2 months later. The readout has drifted PAST the dossier date into August. A July-expiry option would NOT clear the actual catalyst. Require an expiry of Sept-2026 or later (+1 monthly past an August readout). Equity avoids this entirely.

conditions: Express via equity, OR options expiring Sept-2026 or later (NOT July) to clear the slipped August LUGANO readout

passed.passed_because: Edge is negligible on merit, independent of headroom: ev_pct 0.0165 (payoff 0.86, barely positive) and live recompute compressed the upside leg further (FV $36 = +45.5% vs floor $8 = -67.7%, a true coin-flip with sub-1x net payoff). Would skip even with a free lane + driver seat. (Also double-capped: bio lane 5/5 and FDA_clinical_readout 2/2.)

Edge collapsed on the +12.8% run to $24.35: R:R flipped from ~2.3:1 to roughly symmetric (~+23% upside / ~-26% downside, ~0.9:1). Entry is chasing here; undated manufacturing re-inspection (staging) with no enforced date. Skip on merit regardless of headroom. (Also double-capped: bio lane 5/5 and FDA_approval_decision 2/2.)

FDA_clinical_readout driver is 2/2 full (held CELC + AMLX) AND it is a true coin-flip (win_prob 0.45) competing with OLMA/RLMD for the same single hypothetical freed clinical-readout seat; on merit it ranks below VIR/PVLA/AVIR already on the watchlist, so it would be displaced even if the lane freed. Skip rather than on-deck.

FDA_clinical_readout driver 2/2 full; edge eroded most in batch on the +16.3% run (R:R fell ~2.4:1 -> ~1.4:1), needs a limit <= $6.00 to be viable, and option market is unverified on a $6 small-cap. Marginal even on merit; behind the on-deck clinical names. Skip.

Score 4, micro-cap (~$513M), win_prob 0.45 coin-flip; FDA_approval_decision driver 2/2 full. Equity-only, no options edge. Weaker than BBIO/ZYME for any hypothetical freed approval seat. Skip on merit.

Score 4, M-grade, win_prob 0.45; dossier itself flags 'no near-term event to size around' (undated Q4-2026 durability play). FDA_clinical_readout driver 2/2 full and it ranks below the watchlist clinical names. Undated theta risk even via liquid options. Skip on merit.

director.memo:
picks is EMPTY by hard-cap necessity, not weak survivors. Re-checked every combined cap: the bio_convergence lane is already 5/5 FULL from the held book (CELC, VRDN, AQST, WVE, AMLX), which walls off ALL 15 bio_convergence survivors regardless of their cluster headroom (FDA/biotech still had 26.5 wt-pts free, but the lane name-cap binds first). The only two non-bio survivors, MGNI and CLVT, both carry driver Forced_divest_flow, which is 2/2 FULL (held FIG + BLCO). So every one of the 17 survivors is blocked by a combined cap; seating any would breach the lane or the driver cap and be rejected downstream. Forcing a seat is explicitly disallowed, so the basket adds nothing this cycle and the held 10-seat / 55.5%-invested book stands.

Watchlist (10, on-deck): two forced-seller names lead — CLVT (H-grade, R:R improved to ~4.9:1, would enter first when FIG/BLCO frees the Forced_divest_flow driver) and MGNI behind it. The remaining eight are the strongest bio binaries, ranked by win-prob-adjusted edge: BBIO (cleanest, ev 0.45 / R:R 4.4:1 / dated PDUFA), EYPT (highest convexity, ev 1.16), ZYME (win 0.88, tight floor), then VIR / PVLA / AVIR / DBVT / GLUE. Every bio name is double-capped (lane 5/5 plus its FDA driver 2/2), so each would_enter_if requires a held biotech to resolve and free BOTH the lane seat and the matching driver — the natural unlock is CELC/AMLX (clinical) or VRDN/AQST (approval) resolving.

Passed (7): DFTX (ev 0.0165, sub-1x net payoff — skip on merit), RARE (R:R flipped to symmetric ~0.9:1 on the run-up, chasing), and five clinical/approval names (ANNX, OLMA, RLMD, CERS, NKTR) that are coin-flips (win 0.40-0.45) or undated theta-traps ranking below the watchlist names for any single hypothetical freed seat. Shape rationale: the sleeve is deliberately not stretching the FDA/biotech cluster's remaining weight headroom because the lane name-cap — not the weight cap — is the true governor on bio binaries, and that cap is exhausted. The next live decision point is the first held-book resolution, which mechanically unlocks the on-deck queue in driver order.
